corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In this section, we analyze the overestimations of DQN and show that Double DQN improves over DQN both in terms of value accuracy and in terms of policy quality.

context_window.previous: The goal is to get most of the benefit of Double Q-learning, while keeping the rest of the DQN algorithm intact for a fair com- parison, and with minimal computational overhead.
context_window.current: In this section, we analyze the overestimations of DQN and show that Double DQN improves over DQN both in terms of value accuracy and in terms of policy quality.
context_window.next: To further test the robustness of the approach we additionally evaluate the algorithms with random starts generated from expert human trajectories, as proposed by Nair et al.

location.sentence: In some ways, this setting is a best-case scenario for Q-learning, because the deep neural network provides flexible function approx- imation with the potential for a low asymptotic approxima- tion error, and the determinism of the environments prevents the harmful effects of noise.

context_window.previous: DQN combines Q-learning with a flexible deep neural network and was tested on a varied and large set of deterministic Atari 2600 games, reaching human-level performance on many games.
context_window.current: In some ways, this setting is a best-case scenario for Q-learning, because the deep neural network provides flexible function approx- imation with the potential for a low asymptotic approxima- tion error, and the determinism of the environments prevents the harmful effects of noise.
context_window.next: Perhaps surprisingly, we show that even in this comparatively favorable setting DQN some- times substantially overestimates the values of the actions.

location.sentence: In addition, Thrun and Schwartz give a concrete example in which these overes- timations even asymptotically lead to sub-optimal policies, and show the overestimations manifest themselves in a small toy problem when using function approximation.

context_window.previous: Overoptimism due to estimation errors Q-learning’s overestimations were first investigated by Thrun and Schwartz (1993), who showed that if the action values contain random errors uniformly distributed in an in- terval [ − ,  ] then each target is overestimated up to γ m − 1 m +1 , where m is the number of actions.
context_window.current: In addition, Thrun and Schwartz give a concrete example in which these overes- timations even asymptotically lead to sub-optimal policies, and show the overestimations manifest themselves in a small toy problem when using function approximation.
context_window.next: Later van Hasselt (2010) argued that noise in the environment can lead to overestimations even when using tabular representation, and proposed Double Q-learning as a solution.

top_candidates.sentence: In this section, we analyze the overestimations of DQN and show that Double DQN improves over DQN both in terms of value accuracy and in terms of policy quality.
In this section, we analyze the overestimations of DQN and show that Double DQN improves over DQN both in terms of value accuracy and in terms of policy quality.
The popular Q-learning algorithm is known to overestimate action values under certain conditions.
In particular, we first show that the recent DQN algorithm, which combines Q-learning with a deep neural network, suffers from substantial overestimations in some games in the Atari 2600 domain.
We then show that the idea behind the Double Q-learning algorithm, which was introduced in a tabular setting, can be generalized to work with large-scale function approximation.

We propose a specific adaptation to the DQN algorithm and show that the resulting algorithm not only reduces the observed overestimations, as hypothesized, but that this also leads to much better performance on several games.

We propose a specific adaptation to the DQN algorithm and show that the resulting algorithm not only reduces the observed overestimations, as hypothesized, but that this also leads to much better performance on several games.
The goal is for a single algorithm, with a fixed set of hyperparameters, to learn to play each of the games separately from interaction given only the screen pixels as input.
Good solutions must therefore rely heavily on the learning algorithm -it is not practically feasible to overfit the domain by relying only on tuning.
We closely follow the experimental setting and network architecture outlined by .
Briefly, the network architecture is a convolutional neural network with 3 convolution layers and a fully-connected hidden layer (approximately 1.5M parameters in total).
On each game, the network is trained on a single GPU for 200M frames, or approximately 1 week.

Fourth, we have proposed a specific implementation called Double DQN, that uses the existing architecture and deep neural network of the DQN algorithm without requiring additional networks or parameters.

Fourth, we have proposed a specific implementation called Double DQN, that uses the existing architecture and deep neural network of the DQN algorithm without requiring additional networks or parameters.
